site_title: Chrisando E. Pramudhita — Full-Stack Software Developer
site_description: Full-stack software developer in South Tangerang, Indonesia, building fast, accessible websites and web apps for businesses.
footer_body: I would love to help you build faster and more accessible websites.
home_hero_pitch: I'm a full-stack software developer in South Tangerang, Indonesia. I help businesses build fast, accessible websites and web apps.
home_projects_body: I love building and shipping products, so I try to learn things that allows me to fully ship a product on my own. Below are some recent projects I've been working on.
home_project_obserfy_description: Record keeping and parent communication tool for Montessori Schools.
home_project_joyful_description: Marketing site and blog for a Montessori Preschool. Built for speed and high conversion.
home_project_sekitarmu_description: A platform for discovering local small and medium businesses in a Community.
home_project_atreus_description: Automated asset discovery service built for trying out bug bounty.
home_notes_vibe_coding_description: Slides, live demo, source code, and practical resources from the Vibe Coding workshop.
home_open_source_body: Most of my projects are open-sourced and I try to contribute back to the tools that I use when I can. Check out my contributions on GitHub!
about_description: Learn about Chrisando’s background, product mindset, and practical approach to building fast, accessible websites and web apps.
about_heading: I build practical software from idea to launch.
about_intro: I'm Chrisando, an independent full-stack software developer based in South Tangerang, Indonesia. I build websites and web apps for businesses and maintain open-source projects.
about_build_body: My work spans marketing and e-commerce sites, web applications, internal tools, analytics, Android apps, and open-source products. That range helps me make practical decisions from the interface through deployment.
about_work_body: I start by clarifying the problem, the people using the product, and what a useful result looks like. From there, I choose straightforward technology, ship in small steps, and test the real experience as it takes shape.
about_values_body: A finished product should be fast, accessible, understandable, and maintainable. I treat those qualities as part of the work from the beginning, not as a cleanup step before launch.
about_contact_body: Tell me what you are trying to improve or launch. I can help turn it into a focused website or web application that is ready for real users.
cv_description: Explore Chrisando E. Pramudhita’s full-stack software development experience, selected projects, education, and professional training.
cv_exp_independent_1: Designed and deployed e-commerce and marketing sites for SMEs using NextJS and Gatsby.
cv_exp_independent_2: Redesigned internal CRM for managing magazine subscriptions and printing shipping addresses for a publishing company.
cv_exp_independent_3: Helped develop analytic dashboard using React.
cv_exp_independent_4: Developed android app for internal reporting of offsite maintenance result for a building automation company.
cv_exp_bca_1: Implemented new business rules on IBM z/OS.
cv_exp_bca_2: Helped test and monitor new codes to be deployed.
cv_exp_bca_3: Explored, experimented, and benchmarked technologies to adopt.
cv_project_atreus_body: Open-source automated asset discovery service to experiment with bug bounty. Built with NextJS and Go.
cv_project_obserfy_body: Open-source record keeping and parent communication tool for Montessori Schools.
cv_project_portal_body: Open-source, offline-first android app for keeping track of campus info and activities with over 20k downloads.
cv_edu_binus_body: Graduated with GPA of 3.4 and enrolled in the "Global Class" that uses an internationalized curriculum delivered in english.
cv_edu_nsysu_body: Learned Python and UNIX through building and debugging various unix based text games.
cv_course_dl_1: Learned how convolutional neural networks work.
cv_course_dl_2: Learned how to structure machine learning projects.
cv_course_dl_3: Learned about neural networks and deep learning.
cv_course_dl_4: Learned how to optimize deep neural networks.
cv_course_ml_body: Learned various Machine Learning methodologies such as SVM and neural networks using Matlab.
cv_course_gle_body: Leadership development program focused on brainstorming about real world problems.

vibe_link_codex_description: Build and iterate with an AI coding agent.
vibe_summary_what_p1: Vibe coding means building software that actually works just by saying what you want in everyday language. An AI agent, a program that can carry out tasks on its own, writes and runs the code. You never have to type code at all, you just describe the result you want.
vibe_summary_what_p2: The term itself is brand new. It first appeared in early 2025, February 2025 to be exact, when Andrej Karpathy, a research scientist on OpenAI's founding team, described on Twitter how he codes now. He hands all the details over to the agent: "give in to the vibes, embrace exponentials, and forget that the code even exists." In short, a new way of making software: the human directs, the AI agent executes.
vibe_summary_why_p1: The way software gets made is being completely overhauled right now. We no longer need to write code by hand. Many companies have already started to stop coding manually and rely on AI agents for everything.
vibe_summary_why_p2: Working this way, you start moving about 10x faster. A prototype that used to take weeks now takes shape in hours. Work that normally takes a month can be done in under a week. A project that used to need a team of 10 can be handled by 2-3 people, and the quality of the software actually goes up, not down.
vibe_summary_why_p3: And this will not stop at software. The same AI agents work for research, planning, reports, analysis, and monitoring, so the same overhaul is coming to every industry. The bottom line: AI agents are hugely influential, and very much worth learning.
vibe_summary_pace_p1: The pace of change is easier to see for yourself than to take on faith. A research group, METR (metr.org), measures it concretely: how long a task an AI model can complete with about a 50 percent success rate. That number has been doubling roughly every six months.
vibe_summary_pace_p2: To make that concrete, here is the progression. GPT-2 could only handle tasks of about 3 seconds, the level of answering a single quick question. GPT-3.5, the model behind the first version of ChatGPT, reached about 36 seconds. GPT-4 reached about 4 minutes, enough to write a whole file of code.
vibe_summary_pace_p3: Then close to the start of 2025, o3 broke through to about 2 hours. That is roughly the level of building a simple to-do list app that actually works, and this is when truly modern AI agents appeared, the same moment Karpathy named vibe coding. Today GPT-5.2 sits at about 5.9 hours, roughly a multi-user to-do list with login and stored data. GPT-5.6 Sol is projected to reach about 16 hours in 2026, a full-featured to-do list with plenty of extras. The growth is still exponential, with no sign of slowing down.
vibe_summary_spectrum_heading: You do not need to know code to start
vibe_summary_spectrum_p1: To start vibe coding, you honestly do not need to understand code. In its purest form, you write no code at all and rely on the AI agent for every decision. For now, though, there is a limit, because agents still make mistakes and still depend on you to give the right instructions. It still feels like talking to a genie that does not quite understand what you actually want.
vibe_summary_spectrum_p2: So with no coding knowledge at all, projects stay on the smaller side, roughly the scale of a prototype that takes 1-3 weeks. A landing page or an e-commerce store is well covered. A software product, where the product really is the software, can work too, but keep it simple.
vibe_summary_spectrum_p3: Beyond that, you start to need software engineering knowledge. Software has to be maintained and will keep changing, so you have to explain precisely what needs to change so the agent does not change the wrong thing. The longer a project runs, the more carefully you have to make sure security, performance, and privacy all stay intact. And once your users really grow, you will definitely need to understand code more, because scaling up demands it.
vibe_summary_spectrum_p4: At this level you still have to get into the program yourself, but you rely on AI agents to move much faster: you treat the agent like a very smart coworker, delegate work to it, and still understand and review what it produces. People have started calling this agentic engineering: the engineering is done through AI agents instead of by hand.
vibe_summary_agent_heading: What an AI agent actually is
vibe_summary_agent_p1: An AI agent is an AI system that works on its own to finish a task. It runs in a loop: act, evaluate the result, adapt, try again, repeating until the task is done. To do that it uses tools such as a browser, a terminal (a text-based way of controlling a computer), and your files.
vibe_summary_agent_p2: This is what separates an agent from ChatGPT and other chatbots. A chatbot is just a chat exchange: you send a message, it replies, and that is it. An agent keeps working on its own after you give it an instruction, and it can essentially do anything a human can do on a computer: edit documents, analyze data, do research. Claude and Codex are both agents in this sense.
